title: feature_graph manifest shape
purpose: An explicit, hand-built map from a product feature to the code, routes, tests, tables, docs, audit findings, and next actions that define it. One manifest = one feature. A developer opens it and sees everything that governs that feature in one place (audit/june30/26-graph-view-projection.md, audit/june30/27-crm-meta-projection-quality-visibility.md 'IDE View').
law: Every path under files/tests/docs MUST exist on disk. Every route MUST exist in server.js. A test that does not exist is kept but marked {"missing": true}. Facts a sibling agent has not yet posted are marked {"pending": true} with a note (never invent key names). Citation law: each claim cites the file behind it (CLAUDE.md THE CITATION LAW).

fields.feature: string -- human name of the feature.
fields.id: string -- stable slug, matches the manifest file name.
fields.summary: string -- one-line description of what the feature does.
fields.files: array of { path, role } -- source modules/tools/packs that implement the feature. path is repo-relative and must exist.
fields.routes: array of { method, path, server_line, summary } -- HTTP routes in server.js that expose the feature. Each must exist in server.js at the cited line.
fields.tests: array of { path, role, missing? } -- test files that protect the feature. Mark missing:true if the file does not exist.
fields.tables: array of { name, defined_in, role } -- database tables the feature reads or writes. defined_in is the file with the CREATE TABLE statement.
fields.docs: array of { path, role, pending? } -- docs that describe the feature. path must exist unless pending:true.
fields.audit_findings: array of { source, finding } -- relevant findings from the June 30 audit. source is an existing audit file.
fields.next_actions: array of { action, status, note? } -- what to build next. status one of done|open|pending. pending means it depends on a sibling agent's in-flight work.
